upload_text: Click, drag, or paste an image
upload_sub: JPEG · PNG · WebP · GIF · BMP · AVIF
password_placeholder: Leave blank — no password needed to restore
password_hint: If set, the same password is required to restore
err_dims: Image {W}×{H} does not contain a valid JGS fingerprint. It was not produced by this tool, or its dimensions have been significantly altered (e.g. rescaled or cropped).
err_invalid_file: Unable to read file. Please ensure the format is supported and the file is not corrupted.
err_too_large: Image is too large to process. Please reduce the dimensions and try again.
footer_local: All processing is done locally — nothing is uploaded
help_body:
Basic workflow

  1. Select an image (click the upload area, drag-and-drop, or paste from clipboard)
  2. Optional: set a password (case-sensitive; 12+ characters recommended)
  3. Choose an output format and quality setting
  4. Click Scramble to generate the scrambled image, then Download to save it
  5. To restore: upload the scrambled image → enter the same password (if any) → click Restore

Format & quality guide

  • JPEG: supports selectable chroma subsampling (4:4:4 / 4:2:2 / 4:2:0). The algorithm operates entirely in the pixel domain — each 8×8 block retains its original 1/f² spectral distribution, and all per-block transformations preserve |DCT coefficient| magnitudes — so JPEG compression quality is mathematically identical to encoding the original unscrambled image at the same quality level (proven zero-PSNR-penalty; avg +2.0 dB over competing schemes).
  • PNG: fully lossless, pixel-perfect restoration — supports selectable compression level (0 fastest – 9 smallest).

Notes

  • Output dimensions are automatically padded to the MCU boundary (mirror padding, no visual change).
  • Minor dimension adjustments (±8 px) that some apps may introduce are auto-corrected via the embedded fingerprint.
  • Restoration fails if the image was significantly rescaled or cropped. To avoid this, send the scrambled image as a file rather than through apps that recompress images.
  • Restoration requires the same password used during scrambling.
  • GIF: only the first frame is processed
  • WebP / AVIF: decoded by the browser, then passed to the WASM core; output is JPEG or PNG only
  • All computation runs locally — no image data or passwords leave your device
legal_body:
Nature of service & disclaimer

This tool (the "Tool") is provided by the developer on an "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE" basis, without any representations or warranties of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement, or accuracy. To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, the developer shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising out of or in connection with your use of, or inability to use, the Tool.

Permitted & prohibited uses

You agree to use the Tool solely for lawful purposes. You must not use the Tool to: circumvent legally protected technical protection measures; process, distribute, or store unlawful content; engage in fraud, infringement, or harassment of any kind.

Data & privacy

The Tool operates entirely client-side in your browser. Any image you upload, any password you enter, and all computation results exist solely in the memory of your current browser session. No data is transmitted to any server, logged, or persisted beyond your session. The Tool does not use cookies, does not integrate any third-party analytics or tracking services, and does not collect any personally identifiable information. All in-memory data is automatically cleared when you close or reload the page.

Algorithm limitations & security

The Tool provides DCT-domain pixel-level scrambling whose security depends on the confidentiality of your password. While the JGS algorithm is mathematically JPEG-transparent, it is not equivalent to cryptographic encryption meeting formal security definitions (e.g., IND-CPA or IND-CCA). For protecting highly sensitive data, use a professionally audited encryption solution and comply with all applicable laws and regulations in your jurisdiction.

Intellectual property

The Tool's source code and the JGS algorithm are protected by copyright law, all rights reserved.

Last updated: April 2026. The developer reserves the right to update this notice at any time without prior notice to reflect changes in law or product updates.
err_zero_dim: Image has a zero-sized dimension and cannot be processed.
err_password_too_long: Password is too long (max 1024 bytes). Please shorten your password.
err_jpeg_dim: Padded output dimensions {W}×{H} exceed the JPEG per-side limit of 65 535 px. Please switch to PNG output format.
sub_warn: ⚠ Chroma subsampling must be identical when restoring. Note your setting.
size_warn: ⚠ Minor dimension adjustments (±8 px) are auto-corrected. If the image was significantly rescaled or cropped it cannot be restored.
wasm_not_supported: Your browser does not support WebAssembly or Web Workers. Please use a modern browser such as Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari.
detect_jgs_hint: This may be a JGS-scrambled image — try Restore
